                                                               Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100389



                                                               Contents lists available at ScienceDirect


                                                        Egyptian Informatics Journal
                                                      journal homepage: www.sciencedirect.com




Machine learning algorithms in proactive decision making for handover
management from 5G & beyond 5G
A. Priyanka a,⇑, P. Gauthamarayathirumal b, C. Chandrasekar a
a
    Department of Computer Science, Periyar University, Salem, Tamil Nadu, India
b
    Department of Computer Science, Government Arts College, Dharmapuri, Tamil Nadu, India




a r t i c l e          i n f o                          a b s t r a c t

Article history:                                        In recent years, heterogeneous networks (HetNets) have drawn a lot of attention to connecting devices
Received 13 September 2022                              that will enable everything to become smart, efficient, and fast. These networks are made up of many cell
Revised 27 June 2023                                    types, including macro, micro, pico, and femto that are added to suit customer demand. HetNets requires
Accepted 4 July 2023
                                                        sophisticated mobility management to handle a variety of inter-frequency technologies. Mobility man-
Available online 13 July 2023
                                                        agement needs to be adequately addressed to prevent service degradation caused by high rates of unnec-
                                                        essary handover attempts (HOA), handover ping-pong (HOPP), handover failure (HOF), radio link failure
Keywords:
                                                        (RLF) and HO delay involved, which necessitates the user to execute the handover (HO) process while
Proactive decision making
5G
                                                        moving from one place to another. A well-suited HO management technique is proposed to resolve the
Handover                                                issues observed when the user moves. The purpose of this study is to ascertain how the handover control
Handover control parameter                              parameter (HCP) involves the functionality of the 5G network. The novel approach taken into consider-
Machine learning                                        ation in this work for cell selection is proactive decision-making (PDM). The performance of the proposed
                                                        technique is evaluated through a simulation consisting of 5G Hetnets. Comparisons of evaluations were
                                                        made in terms of HOA, HOPP, HOF, RLF and HO delay.
                                                         Ó 2023 THE AUTHORS. Published by Elsevier BV on behalf of Faculty of Computers and Artificial Intel-
                                                           ligence, Cairo University. This is an open access article under the CC BY-NC-ND license (http://creative-
                                                                                                                               commons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/).




1. Introduction                                                                          ders and users are dealing with the management of HO in 5G tech-
                                                                                         nologies and face several difficulties in implementing seamless
    Wireless communication technologies are experiencing                                 connections. The first issue is inaccurate signal measurement,
remarkable growth by seamlessly interconnecting everything,                              which is made more difficult by the high mmWave frequency
enabling efficient and rapid innovations. The Internet of Things                         and bandwidth needed to boost the network communication sys-
(IoT), augmented reality (AR), virtual reality (VR), automated driv-                     tem’s capacity. Due to atmospheric factors, little diffraction around
ing assistance systems (ADAS), high-definition video streaming,                          walls or other obstructions, and rain absorption, the signal path
and emerging applications all contribute to the researchers’ efforts                     loss increases in the high-frequency bands. Furthermore, fading
in constructing a smarter world to ensure uninterrupted device                           can readily affect signals operating in the high-frequency range,
management and unbreakable connections. Among the various                                leading to errors that diminish the total switching rate of HO.
advancements in mobile technology, the most noteworthy advan-                            Moreover, it will reduce the customer’s experience. However, as
tage of 5G is its ability to deliver significantly faster speeds. This                   with any new technology, some challenges must be addressed to
implies that 5G can greatly enhance the user experience in terms                         ensure its success. One such challenge is handover management,
of file downloads and film streaming (Fig. 1).                                           which refers to the process of transferring an ongoing communica-
    As the world transitions towards a more connected future, the                        tion session from one base station to another as a user moves
need for efficient and seamless communication networks becomes                           between coverage areas.
increasingly important. The 5th generation of mobile networks                               Traditionally, the measurement gap (MG) occurs when the end
promises to revolutionize communication by enabling faster data                          user (EU) measures the radio parameter of the target cell during
transfer rates, lower latency, and better connectivity. Service provi-                   the HO process [1]. The idea of continuous high-speed data transfer
                                                                                         in next-generation mobile communications conflicts with the mea-
                                                                                         surement gap (MG) because data transmission pauses and causes
    ⇑ Corresponding author.                                                              an undesired latency. MGs are critical periods where transmission
      E-mail address: venkatpriya57@gmail.com (A. Priyanka).

temporarily stops and even call drops may occur. It might be diffi-           aspect when accessing programs available across different net-
cult to choose the best network from a heterogeneous network                  works. This task becomes more challenging in heterogeneous net-
with the least amount of MG, yet doing so is essential to maintain-           works due to fluctuating network traffic caused by real-time and
ing the quality of service in these networks. For a heterogeneous             general applications. To tackle this problem, the study proposes a
system, we suggested a network selection strategy in this study.              machine-learning (ML) based plan that selects the best network
The design objective is to offer its devoted customers the greatest           among several coexisting networks at a specific location.
network possible in terms of Quality of Service (QoS). Network
selection is based on dependability, reputation, and network QoS                The main objective is to develop an approach that intelligently
parameters. A network’s dependability and reputation are crucial                 predicts and selects the most suitable network for handover
for the user experience. The next generation of heterogeneous                    before it occurs. This aims to reduce time consumption during
wireless networks is user-centric and gives users the choice to                  handover processes and improve user experience by seamlessly
switch service providers based on their quality of experience. The               connecting to the optimal network.
precise strategy considered in this work will characteristic nicely             The next goal is to establish a mathematical model that accu-
for cell selection. It is referred to as PDM. Proactive choice suggests          rately predicts the appropriate values of handover control
that cell selection is not finished at the time of the HO cause.                 parameters based on real-time network conditions. This opti-
Instead, the counseled method will pick the suitable cell proac-                 mization aims to minimize disruptions, improve network selec-
tively and take that cell into account when HO commences. HCP                    tion decisions, and ensure smoother handover transitions.
plays a crucial role in ensuring smooth handover transitions and                Another importance is to integrate edge-based data storage into
minimizing disruptions to communication. In this research article,               5G handover management. This involves leveraging the capabil-
we investigate the impact of handover control parameters on 5G                   ities of edge computing to store and process data closer to the
handover management and propose strategies for optimizing their                  user, reducing latency and improving response times during
values to enhance the overall quality of service for 5G networks.                handovers.

1.1. Research objectives                                                      1.2. Research contribution

    The countries are aiming to upgrade their telecommunications                 The research paper explores three significant contributions in
systems to 5G and beyond 5G (B5G) for technological advance-                  the context of 5G handover management: Proactive Decision Mak-
ment. One critical aspect of these systems is efficient Handover              ing (PDM) to reduce time consumption, Handover Control Param-
(HO) management to ensure uninterrupted communication. Previ-                 eter (HCP) for network selection, and the utilization of edge-based
ous approaches utilizing novel weight-based network assessment                data storage.
methods have revealed unnecessary HO operations. The perfor-
mance of each cell can vary significantly due to the nature of net-             Firstly, the introduction of PDM aims to minimize time con-
working technologies, leading to non-essential HO operations. To                 sumption during the handover process. PDM involves the intel-
address this, a fresh weight consideration-based HO execution                    ligent prediction and selection of the most suitable network for
approach is proposed to minimize unnecessary operations. Out-                    seamless connectivity before the actual handover occurs. By
dated techniques have exposed the MG issue, highlighting the need                proactively analyzing these two parameters for evaluation, like
for a time-efficient schema in the context of 5G. However, measur-               arbitrary strength unit (ASU) and network download rate
ing the parameter values to select an optimal cell requires addi-                (NDR), PDM enables faster and more efficient network selection,
tional time, especially when considering multiple characteristics                leading to reduced handover time and improved user
during the HO commencement. Network selection is a crucial                       experience.

sequence is predicted, and the HO process is carried out.                    user experience in performance.

process is a hindrance, the cell selection process will be concluded         troller’s solution [14]. Using a cutting-edge state-space aggregation

A. Priyanka, P. Gauthamarayathirumal and C. Chandrasekar                                                       Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100389


When training a neural network, it is critical that the data in the         3. Proposed model
training set fulfill the independent and identical distribution. In
contrast, there is a link between the training concentration data                The working model for simulation makes use of a HetNets de-
that the reinforcement learning sampling has found. If this data            sign that consists of several macro cells, micro cells, pico cells,
is exploited for consecutive training, the neural network might             and femtocells that are evenly distributed over the entire system
become unstable. DQN employs an experience replay technique                 following the needs of the user. EUs are generated at random
to disrupt the correlation between the data to address this                 throughout all locations, with random mobility models across all
issue.                                                                      cells, and they are free to go anywhere in the geographic region
    The Handover Control Parameter (HCP) parameters, which may              throughout time t. We determine the EU as E = {e1, e2 .............ep}.
be manually or automatically adjusted in [17], control the HO man-          Meanwhile, E represents the set of EUs where e e E can travel in
agement issue. In this study, the effect of various HCP settings on         a random direction He e [0, 2 p] and the average velocity of the
the functionality of 5G networks is examined. The report provides           EU Ve e [vmin, vmax]. The description for the macro cell is given as
a comprehensive overview of how current research identifies the             M = {m1, m2.............mq} while the microcell is S = {s1, s2.............sr}
most straightforward approach to dealing with HO. The experi-               and pico cell is P = {p1,p2.............pu} and femtocell is F = {f1,
ment’s results show that there are trade-offs between different             f2.............fv}.
systems’ results. When comparing lower HCP settings to higher                    If the EU switches from the serving cell to the destination cell,
HCP settings, Outage probability benefits are greatly increased.            the HO operation might be completed in the same kind of cell or
Simultaneously, lesser HCP settings have significant drawbacks in           a new one. Based on the measurement data provided by the EU,
terms of high Ping-Pong handover probability for all scenarios of           the serving cell decides in this case about HO issues. Each cell pro-
mobile speed when compared to higher HCP values. The simula-                vides a trustworthy radio link to the EU within their particular
tion results indicate that if one of these systems is employed, mid-        broadcast area. With the value of Reference Signal Received Power
range HCP settings may be the best option. The best user                    (RSRP), the radio link’s quality is determined. The Network Down-
experience-related strategy, according to this study, is the adop-          load Rate (NDR) is manually set in each EU, according to the RSRP.
tion of automatic self-optimisation (ASO) elements.                         All cells have their Handover Control Parameter (HCP) settings

research efforts are defining drones. A brief discussion and recom-
mendations for future research paths on connected drones in                    The devices will reinforce the connection through the initiation
future heterogeneous networks are also provided by this survey.             of the HO process when they venture beyond the transmission
    In this observation, they included a velocity-based self-               range of the current cell. As the devices move away from the cur-
optimization approach to adjust the HO control parameters in                rent cell, the detected signal strength may weaken, potentially
4G/5G networks [22]. The advised approach adjusts the HO margin             impacting the ongoing data delivery process. In such cases, a stron-
and the time to trigger based totally on the users movement inside          ger signal strength is necessary to ensure efficient data transfer.
the network. The simulation consequences show that the proposed             Thus, HO becomes essential to prevent a decline in performance.
approach achieves a top notch discount inside the charge of ping-           The threshold value acts as an indicator to anticipate a connection
pong HOs and RLF whilst as compared to other existing algorithms,           with lower quality. If the signal strength reading falls below the
surpassing such algorithms by using a median of more than 70% for           designated threshold, the device will promptly activate HO
all HO performance parameters.                                              management.
    The auto-tuning optimization (ATO) technique for HO manage-                By applying the requirements from the EU side, such as when
ment is presented in this study and makes use of user speed and             the serving cell’s received radio link quality falls below the thresh-
received signal reference power [23]. The goal of the suggested             old value taken into consideration in this study, the HO process
approach is to reduce the frequency of HOs and HOF ratios. They -           will be started.
provide a HO self-optimization approach for HetNets inside this
research to enhance network performance. The suggested ATO                  3.3. Problem formulation
algorithm modifies the values of HCPs on a regular basis based
on EU speed and RSRP. Through a two-tier model simulation made                 Devices require a wireless connection to provide services to cli-
up of 4G and 5G networks is assessed. The simulation findings               ents, and multiple cells are required to ensure this connectivity.
reveal that the ATO algorithm enhances overall system perfor-               The EU will assess the quality of the radio link based on the RSRP
mance and outperforms all other comparable methods. This sys-               value. ASU represents an integer value that is inversely related to
tem also decreases the overall rate of all performance measures             the RSRP measured by the mobile device. ASU serves as a measure-
by more than 80% when compared to other cutting-edge algo-                  ment map for RSRP. The efficiency of the radio connection plays a
rithms. Consequently, adapting HCPs to EU situations is a practical         crucial role in the EU’s success. To address handover management
and efficient method for managing mobility.                                 issues using HCP, quantitative counseling involves the utilization
                                                                        5
A. Priyanka, P. Gauthamarayathirumal and C. Chandrasekar                                                   Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100389


of mathematical and statistical techniques to analyze and optimize          surement of the source cell from the EU side is applied every sec-
the transition process. The following steps can be taken to imple-          ond. Before the HO trigger occurs, the EU will collect the HCP
ment quantitative therapy in this scenario:                                 values of all neighbours to choose the target cell. The top-scoring
                                                                            HCP provider will be selected by the EU, and it will be considered
  Identify the Issue: The first stage is to identify the issue that        as the target cell when HO triggers.The information along with a
   needs to be resolved. This could include calculating the HCP             measurement report regarding the target cell is forwarded to the
   value to minimize handover delays or maximize network                    source cell and the source can process the HO management.
   bandwidth.
                                                                            Algorithm
  Data Collection: The next stage involves collecting data, which
   includes ASU and NDR. This information can be obtained
   through the EU.                                                            1. begin
  Data Offloading to Edge: To leverage edge-based data storage,              2. Setup the simulation
   the network selection algorithm can take into account the avail-           a. EU (E = {e1, e2 .............ep})
   ability of edge servers and their capacity for storing and pro-            b. macro cell (M = {m1, m2 .............mq})
   cessing data. When selecting a network, the algorithm can                  c. micro cell (S = {s1, s2 .............sr})
   prioritize networks with nearby edge servers capable of offload-           d. pico cell (P = {p1, p2 .............pu})
   ing data from the EU. This can reduce latency and improve over-            e. femto cell (F = {f1, f2 .............fv})
   all network performance.                                                   3. Start Simulation
  Edge-Based Data Processing: Edge computing can also be uti-                a. Simulation time(t)
   lized for data processing tasks related to network selection. By           4. Start mobility (EU)
   performing calculations and analysis at the edge, the algorithm            a. EU speed Ve  [vmin, vmax]
   can make more informed decisions based on real-time network                5. HCP of every cell can collect the ASU and NDR from the EU
   conditions and user requirements.                                          a. The user experienced data is collected
  Data Analysis: Following the collection of the data, statistical           b. Those are stored and processed in edge closer to the EU
   techniques like polynomial regression can be used to analyze               c. Apply polynomial regression to predict the NDR
   it. This can give insights into the connections between handover           d. The predicted NDR is the final HCP value
   control settings and network performance and assist in identi-             6. EU – collects HCP(N)
   fying patterns and trends in the data. The dataset predicts that           a. IF HCP(N1) > HCP(N2) 8 N
   the NDR will determine network efficiency. Polynomial Regres-              b. N1 is considered as best cell by EU (PDM)
   sion is the technique employed for the forecast procedure. The             7. Apply RSRP measurement at EU side
   independent value is ASU, and the dependent value is NDR.                  8. if RSRP(S)<RSRP(d)
   The NDR will be predicted for all values of ASU. Finally, the              a. HO trigger
   average of the predicted NDR will be used as the HCP value,                b. Update HOM
   and the network selection procedure will be completed based                9. else (HO Decision – false)
   on these values.                                                           a. Keep a connection to SOURCE CELL
  Validation: Finally, the HCP can be validated using simulation to          10. end
   ensure that it provides the promised network speed gains.

   The cell reselection process is triggered when the EU changes its
position. The neighboring cells of the EU can provide HCP values.
Based on this value, the EU selects the best service provider as            4. Simulation environment
the target cell for handover management. Proactively, the EU
chooses the top cell from all the available cells to establish han-            The proposed technique is implemented in OMNET++. This
                                                                            research work is an extension of our previous work [24], and there
dover management. According to the proposed strategy, the best
cells are selected prior to the activation of handover. After han-          are some drawbacks observed when we consider this model to 5G
                                                                            HetNets. The presented new model works well for future technolo-
dover triggers, the identified cell becomes the target cell, and han-
dover management is conducted following the established                     gies. 5G network simulations were used to assess and validate the
procedures.                                                                 proposed technique. The average values obtained from 300 EUs
                                                                            represent all outcomes included in the measurements throughout
   Table 3 below displays the HCP value that perhaps the EU
received for the network reselection procedure in our simulation.           this work. Initially, each user had a different set of random coordi-
                                                                            nates in the cell. Although EU mobility is focused in one way, each
Here, the EU considers five cells to be neighbours to choose the
best cell again. The five cells’ measurement reports are evaluated,         EU used to have an exceptional path that became parallel to some
                                                                            other user’s degree. All mobile device users throughout the net-
and the top-scoring cells are chosen for the HO procedure. The
EU chooses F2 as its target cell since it is this situation’s best-         work who were only ever allowed to go in a random direction have
                                                                            already been encouraged to follow the random waypoint mobility
performing cell.
   When the simulation starts, the EU can rottenly change its posi-         model. As a consequence, since performance is assessed separately
                                                                            for each user throughout each simulation period, the findings will
tion by applying a random mobility model. The EU can roam in the
same cell or between different cells. The time duration is fixed for        be more accurate. This is attained in 5 seconds by their movement
                                                                            within the cells, which matches the movement distance with the
the minimum and maximum velocity of the EU. The RSRP mea-

  EU height (m)                                            1.5
  Mobility model                                           Random Waypoint Model
  Simulation time (s)                                      600
  EU speed (meter/second)                                  20,40,60,80,100
  Thermal noise density (dbm/Hz)                           174
  Noise figure of EU (dB)                                  9
  Time to trigger (ms)                                     Adaptive
  Prediction models                                        polynomial regression

                                                                               5.2. HOPP
works. After that, the recommended method was matched with
other algorithms including MADM, Fuzzy MADM, and neural net-
                                                                                  Reducing handover ping pong is important in mobile network-
works. The simulations that were done for the average HOA, HOPP,
                                                                               ing because it helps to optimize network performance, reduce sig-
RLF, HOF and HO delay were obtained by comparing the perfor-
                                                                               naling overhead, and improve user experience. Handover ping
mance of the available approaches. The key parameters used in this
                                                                               pong refers to the situation where a mobile device switches back
simulation are shown in Table 4.
                                                                               and forth between two or more neighboring base stations fre-
                                                                               quently. HOPP is the representation of unnecessary HO happening
5.1. HOA                                                                       between the source and target cell. When the EU disconnects its
                                                                               radio link from the source cell, establishes a new connection with
   In mobile networking, an HOA is a process in which a mobile                 another cell, and then bounces back to the previous source cell, it is
device switches from one base cell to another while maintaining                considered HOPP. This can occur due to a variety of reasons, such
an uninterrupted connection to the network. This process is neces-             as weak signal strength, interference, or incorrect configuration
sary because as the mobile device moves, its signal strength and               of handover parameters.
quality may degrade or change due to various factors such as dis-
tance, obstacles, and interference, which can affect the quality of
the communication. The HOA helps to ensure thate mobile devices
always maintain the best possible connection to the network with-
out any interruptions or dropped calls. But unnecessary HOAs on
mobile networks can cause several issues, including:

  Reduced network performance
  Increased battery consumption
  Dropped calls and poor call quality
  Increased network complexity

  Therefore, it is important for mobile network operators to opti-
mize handover procedures and ensure that they only occur when                                            Fig. 2. Average HOA.

                                                                           7
A. Priyanka, P. Gauthamarayathirumal and C. Chandrasekar                                                   Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100389


                                                                               Fig. 4 shows the common RLF as opposed to different EU speeds
                                                                            accomplished from the simulation. The achieved outcomes of com-
                                                                            mon RLF from the endorsed version turn out to be less than the
                                                                            other models. The examined version notably reduces the common
                                                                            RLF under all speeds of the EU.

                                                                            5.4. HOF

                                                                               The lack of target resource availability is one of the reasons for
                                                                            HOF. When the HO process is initiated, the EU will search the
                                                                            neighbours for target cell selection. There can be a chance that
                             Fig. 3. Average HOPP.
                                                                            insufficient resource availability will lead to the HOF. Another rea-
                                                                            son for HOF is that the EU can move out of the network coverage
          NHOPP                                                            area of the target cell before completely establishing the HO pro-
HOPP ¼                                                           ð2Þ        cess. Reducing handover failure is important in mobile networking
           NRHO
                                                                            because it can impact network performance, user experience, and
   Where NHOPP is some occurred HOPP from the overall simula-               network capacity. Handover failure refers to a situation where a
tion and NRHO could be the number of HOs requested. The average             mobile device fails to complete a handover to a neighboring cell,
HOPP in relation to the EU speeds during the length of the exper-           which can result in dropped calls, poor call quality, and reduced
iment is shown in Fig. 3. According to the simulation findings,             network capacity.
the suggested approach achieves a lower HOPP rate than alterna-
                                                                                   NHOF
tive methods for all EU speeds. As a result, especially when the            HOF ¼                                                                   ð4Þ
EU is moving quickly, the high HOPP rate leads to an increase in                    NHOA
HOAs. Furthermore, a high HOA rate may lead to higher HOPP                     Where NHOF is the count of HOF and NHOA is the number of HO
and HOF rates.                                                              attempted. Fig. 5 shows the unusual HOF perceived from the sim-
                                                                            ulation. The complete consequences of commonplace HOF from
5.3. RLF                                                                    the considered model are less than the alternative models. The
                                                                            examined model significantly reduces the common HOF.
    The connection loss of an EU from the source cell can occur due
to low-quality links during the HO process. Failure of the radio link
                                                                            5.5. HO delay
concurrently with the activity of the HO process, which disrupts
the communication, is treated as RLF. Reducing radio link failure
                                                                               The length of time it takes to establish a connection between
is important in mobile networking because it can impact network             the old cell and the new cell is considered as a delay in the HO pro-
performance, the user experience, and network capacity. RLF refers
                                                                            cess. The process must only take a short amount of time to choose
to the situation where a mobile device is unable to maintain a              a cell, or else the delay will be huge. In comparison to the current
stable connection with the network due to poor signal strength
                                                                            work, the suggested technique performs the HO process in some
or quality. RLF can lead to reduced network capacity and perfor-            kind of a relatively short amount of time. Handover delays can
mance due to increased signaling traffic and dropped calls. RLF
                                                                            decrease network bandwidth and efficiency due to higher signaling
can lead to unnecessary handover attempts, which can consume
network resources and reduce capacity. To reduce RLF, mobile net-
work operators can optimise network parameters such as antenna
placement, transmit power, and handover thresholds. They can
also deploy additional base stations or implement advanced tech-
nologies such as beamforming and carrier aggregation to improve
network coverage and capacity. By reducing RLF, mobile network
operators can improve network performance, increase user satis-
faction, and reduce operational costs. The average probability of
RLF from all the EU is:
        Pp
 
           i¼1 RLF
RLF ¼                                                            ð3Þ
             p

traffic and dropped calls. Network providers can enhance network                  Automation will probably become more prevalent in transfer
efficiency and ease congestion by cutting down on HO delays.                   administration in the future. With the use of AI and machine learn-
    Fig. 6 shows the report’s quick summary and how significantly              ing, networks will be able to identify anomalies and possible issues
the suggested technique minimizing the time duration to change                 autonomously and take corrective action without the need for
over the connection. The time of EU in the new cell (T-new cell)               human involvement. Developing more sophisticated ML models,
and the time of EU in the old cell (T-old cell) have been discovered           such as deep learning or ensemble learning, to improve decision
to measure the handover delay.                                                 accuracy and adaptability and to address privacy and security con-
                                                                               cerns using techniques like privacy-preserving ML or secure feder-
HO Delay ¼ T new cell  T old cell                                  ð5Þ        ated learning will be the scope.

                                                                               Data availability statement
5.6. Limitations of the proposed methodology

                                                                                  Data will be made available by sending a reasonable request to
  Inadequate network coverage: Handover choices based solely
                                                                               the first author. Communication can be done through email to the
   on download rate may fail to take the network’s coverage region
                                                                               provided email address.
   into account. As a consequence, handovers may start even if the
   destination network has poor coverage in the user’s location,
   resulting in call drops.                                                    CRediT authorship contribution statement
  Delayed handover initiation: Handover control settings that are
   not changed regularly may cause delays in handover initiation.                 A. Priyanka: Conceptualization, Methodology, Software, Inves-
   This can be a reason for poor user experience, particularly for             tigation, Resources, Data curation, Writing – original draft, Writing
   apps requiring real-time data transmission.                                 – review & editing, Visualization, Project administration, Funding
  Frequent changes may lead to high signaling overhead after                  acquisition. P. Gauthamarayathirumal: Formal analysis. C. Chan-
   each base station initializes the handover control parameters,              drasekar: Validation, Supervision.
   which will clog the network and impair performance.
                                                                               Declaration of Competing Interest

6. Conclusion                                                                      The authors declare that they have no known competing finan-
                                                                               cial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared
    The importance of HO management is to maintain the QoS for                 to influence the work reported in this paper.
the EU applications during their mobility. The 5G handover
methodology is also expected to provide better coverage and                    Acknowledgments
capacity for mobile networks. This will improve the quality of ser-
vice for mobile users and lead to happier customers. 5G and future                The first author sincerely acknowledges the financial support
technologies are rapidly advancing by outperforming older tech-                (University Research Fellowship) provided by the Department of
nologies in terms of high-speed data rate and low latency. When                Computer Science, Periyar University under the grant: PU/AD-3/
devices move fast, mobility management is a meaningful task to                 URF Selection Order/016175/2020.
strengthen the seamless data delivery process. And the whole pro-
cess of mobility management should be done with minimal                        References
